description: Persistent state for the Daily Closed-Loop Operations Review cron job. Stores proposal/no-action history for deduplication and follow-up tracking.

proposals.title: Fix cron audit trail visibility
proposals.existing_process_anchor: Daily Cron Health Report and cron audit JSONL under /opt/data/logs/cron_runs
proposals.business_reason: The operator-facing health report depends on reliable run evidence. If audit records are missing while jobs are running, Roman may get a falsely quiet or degraded view and lose confidence in whether scheduled operations actually executed.
evidence: The review report shows audit_records_count: 0 for 2026-04-30 at /opt/data/logs/cron_runs/2026-04-30.jsonl.
The same evidence shows cron output files and a successful Daily Cron Health Report run on 2026-04-30, so scheduled activity existed but was not represented in the daily audit record count.

Daily Cron Health Report outputs on 2026-04-30 reported degraded status with 0 observed runs after the script path issue was fixed, indicating the health report may be relying on incomplete audit data.

notes: No prior open proposals existed in the state file, so there was nothing to follow up before this recommendation.

proposals.title: No action recommended today
proposals.business_reason: Yesterday's evidence shows operational noise and follow-up signals, but no clearly stronger, non-duplicative improvement than the existing open audit-trail proposal. Recommending another initiative now would risk distracting from the pending approval item.
evidence: The prior cron audit visibility proposal remains open with status proposed and still needs Roman approval to write a plan.

For 2026-05-01, the cron audit file exists and contains 2 records, while scheduled cron outputs and health reporting continued without delivery errors, so the new signal is mixed rather than a separate strong fix candidate.

Gateway and usage-limit errors appeared in logs, but they are tied to the Hermes update and manual sessions rather than a clear recurring scheduled-process failure requiring a new closed-loop proposal today.

notes: The open cron audit trail visibility proposal was reviewed. New evidence shows the 2026-05-01 audit file has 2 records, which is an encouraging signal, but the item is still pending Roman approval to write an implementation plan or close it after the scheduled follow-up check.
No implemented proposal records exist yet, so there was no implementation outcome to review.

proposals.title: No action recommended today
proposals.business_reason: Yesterday's evidence reinforces the already-open cron audit trail visibility issue, but does not justify a separate new initiative. The highest-value next step remains resolving or approving the existing proposal rather than adding another work item.
evidence: The prior cron audit trail visibility proposal remains open with status proposed and still needs Roman approval to write a plan.

For 2026-05-02, the audit record count is again 0 while scheduled jobs still ran successfully, including Slack Daily Digest, Daily Memory Enrichment, Daily Cron Health Report, and Daily Closed-Loop Operations Review.

Daily Git Auto-Commit reported a push protection failure caused by sensitive material in a local backup commit, but it was investigated in manual sessions and does not create a stronger non-duplicative closed-loop proposal today.

notes: The open cron audit trail visibility proposal was reviewed. The 2026-05-02 evidence shows audit_records_count: 0 again despite scheduled job activity, so the item still appears relevant and still needs Roman approval to write a plan or an explicit decision to close it.
No implemented proposal records exist yet, so there was no implementation outcome to review.

proposals.title: No action recommended today
proposals.business_reason: Yesterday's evidence still points mainly to the already-open cron audit trail visibility issue. Creating another initiative would duplicate the pending item and distract from getting a clear approval or closure decision.
evidence: The prior cron audit trail visibility proposal remains open with status proposed and still needs Roman approval to write a plan.

For 2026-05-03, audit_records_count is 0 while scheduled jobs still show successful last_status values and multiple cron outputs were produced, including Daily Closed-Loop Operations Review, Slack Daily Digest, Daily Memory Enrichment, Weekly Memory Review, Daily Git Auto-Commit, and Daily Cron Health Report.
The Daily Cron Health Report output for the period ending 2026-05-03 reported degraded status with 0 observed runs, reinforcing the same audit-visibility gap rather than a separate new improvement.

notes: The open cron audit trail visibility proposal was reviewed. The 2026-05-03 evidence again shows zero audit records despite successful scheduled job outputs, so the item remains relevant and still needs Roman approval to write a plan or an explicit decision to close it.
No implemented proposal records exist yet, so there was no implementation outcome to review.
